serving.natural_language_portion: A typical hawker serving of Kolo Mee is approximately 300 g (one bowl), containing around 570 kcal, 31 g protein, 23.7 g fat, and 57.8 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Kolo Mee — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for Kolo Mee (cr_id F05060069, confidence 0.95). All seven reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are taken as-is from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous pork noodle dish (SR Legacy #21376) as approximately 2.3 g/100 g. Remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; egg noodle / wheat noodle analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kolo Mee in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 58 (medium) is estimated from the closest analogous food: boiled egg noodles / wheat-based Asian noodles (GI range 47–61, median ~58) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 and the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (58 × 57.84) / 100 ≈ 33.6 for a 300 g serving. The input record cited GI 58 / GL 11.2 (per 100 g basis); GL per serving recalculated here on a 300 g serving basis.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 386 mg/100 g with minimal potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: At 386 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1,158 mg per 300 g serving — nearly 50% of the 2,400 mg daily limit), Kolo Mee poses a high sodium burden for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced soy sauce and oyster sauce, avoid additional condiments, and limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (58) refined egg noodles with very low dietary fibre (0.56 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The medium GI of 58 combined with a high glycemic load per serving (~33.6 for 300 g) and very low fibre (0.56 g/100 g) results in significant postprandial glucose excursion; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce noodle portion, increase vegetable toppings to add fibre, and avoid sweetened char siu glaze.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from lard and char siu, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kolo Mee contains approximately 2.3 g saturated fat per 100 g from lard and BBQ pork, alongside 386 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should request less lard/oil, substitute char siu with lean chicken or tofu, and limit portion size to manage both saturated fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory ingredients: char siu (arachidonic acid), lard (saturated fat), refined noodles (high glycaemic load)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kolo Mee's combination of red meat (char siu), lard, and refined low-fibre noodles creates a pro-inflammatory nutritional profile; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute char siu with chicken or tofu, request less lard, and increase vegetable toppings to reduce inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kolo Mee (干捞面) is a Sarawakian Chinese dry noodle dish originating from Sarawak, Malaysia, and widely popular in Singapore hawker centres. It consists of thin springy egg noodles that are boiled, drained, and tossed in a fragrant mixture of shallot oil, lard, light soy sauce, and oyster sauce — served dry rather than in broth. The dish is typically topped with seasoned minced pork, slices of char siu (Cantonese BBQ pork), and sometimes wonton or fish balls. The texture is springy and silky, with a savoury-umami flavour profile from the soy-oyster sauce base and the aromatic shallot oil. Per 100 g, Kolo Mee provides 190 kcal, 10.33 g protein, 7.9 g fat, 19.28 g carbohydrate, 0.56 g dietary fibre, and 386 mg sodium. The GI is estimated at 58 (medium), yielding a high glycaemic load of approximately 33.6 per 300 g serving due to the refined egg noodle base and low fibre content. Clinically, the dish is a moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes (medium GI, high GL, low fibre), a high concern for hypertension (borderline-high sodium at 386 mg/100 g with minimal potassium offset), and a moderate concern for cardiovascular disease (saturated fat from lard and char siu). It is not halal due to pork content. Common allergens include gluten, egg, soy, and shellfish (oyster sauce). Kolo Mee is a staple breakfast and lunch dish in Sarawak and is found across Singapore hawker centres and Malaysian Chinese coffee shops (kopitiams).
common_user_queries: Is kolo mee suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database (cr_id F05060069, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous pork noodle dish. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous egg noodle data. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology anchored at approximate parent-level codes with honest gap notes.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB Foodo for this entry): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: HPB Foodo reports only energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, and dietary fibre for this entry; detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrients, and moisture data are not published for this specific dish in the HPB database as of 2026. Saturated_fat_g (2.3 g/100 g) is an estimate adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for a comparable pork and noodle dish; this value should be updated when HPB publishes a full fatty acid profile. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on boiled egg/wheat noodles from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Kolo Mee exists in the database as of 2026. Glycemic load per serving recalculated on a 300 g serving basis (GL ≈ 33.6); the input record's GL of 11.2 was on a per-100 g basis. The dish contains pork and is not halal. Kolo Mee is a Sarawakian Chinese dish; multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements.
